mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth signals expanding corporate earnings and economic momentum, which directly increases equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses corporate profit expectations and generates negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates collapsing consumer demand and credit stress, which forces equity markets to price in lower future cash flows over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal yields positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles, a federal funds rate falling below 4.0 percent reduces the discount rate applied to future earnings, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are negative in more than 60 percent of instances when the rate threshold is crossed.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect stronger manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The correlation between industrial production levels and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.05 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Weak nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a rising unemployment rate signals structural labor market deterioration, which reduces aggregate demand and forces equity valuations lower over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance at the 90 percent confidence level in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales exceeding 350 billion dollars indicates robust consumer spending power and economic resilience, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months where retail sales cross the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, the consumer price index exceeding 205 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and causes negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of instances when the inflation index crosses the threshold.
